records.ety: [OE. latis, F. lattis lathwork, fr. latte lath. See Latten, 1st Lath.]

defs: Any work of wood, metal, plastic, or other solid material, made by crossing a series of parallel laths, or thin strips, with another series at a diagonal angle, and forming a network with openings between the strips; as, the lattice of a window; -- called also latticework.
quotes: The mother of Sisera looked out at a window, and cried through the lattice.

defs: The representation of a piece of latticework used as a bearing, the bands being vertical and horizontal.

The arrangement of atoms or molecules in a crystal, represented as a repeating arrangement of points in space, each point representing the location of an atom or molecule; called also crystal lattice and space lattice.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Latticed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Latticing (?).]

defs: To make a lattice of; as, to lattice timbers.
To close, as an opening, with latticework; to furnish with a lattice; as, to lattice a window.
quotes: Therein it seemeth he [Alexander] hath latticed up Caesar.
